subject: Physics
solution:
(A) A Klystron valve or the magnetron valve are generally used for the production of electromagnetic waves and that electromagnetic wave is Microwave. So, A→IV.

(B) Gamma rays are produced during the disintegration of radioactive atomic nuclei and the decay of certain subatomic particles.So, B→I

(C) Radio waves can be produced by rapid acceleration and deceleration of electrons in aerials (conductors). These can be detected by receivers of aerials. So, C→II

(D) X-rays are produced when a metal target or any other element is bombarded with high energy particles such as electrons, photons or ions. When high energy electrons is incident on a bound electron in an atom, then bound electron emits from the inner shell of that atom. So, D→III

subject: Physics
solution: The electromagnetic (EM) spectrum is the range of all types of EM radiation. The γ‐ray has a maximum frequency or minimum wavelength; since the wavelength is inversely proportional to frequency, while radio wave has a minimum frequency and maximum wavelength. The order of wavelength will be; Gamma rays G < X-rays X < Ultra violet U < Visible rays V < Infrared rays I < Microwaves  M < Radio waves R.

The speed of electromagnetic waves in a medium is given by v=1με  ...i

where μ and ε are absolute permeability and absolute permittivity of the medium.
Now, μ=μ0μr and ε=ε0εr
Then, we have, 

v=1μ0μr×ε0εr=1μ0ε0×1μrεr=cμrεr

orεr=c2v2μr=3×10822×1082=94=2.25

subject: Physics
Physics
solution:
Theoretical
A-II, B-III, C-IV, D-I

A. $\oint \vec{E} \cdot d\vec{l} = -\frac{d}{dt} \oint \vec{B} \cdot d\vec{a}$ → Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction (II)
B. $\oint \vec{B} \cdot d\vec{l} = \mu_0 \left(I + \epsilon_0 \frac{d\Phi_E}{dt}\right)$ → Ampere-Maxwell law (III)
C. $\oint \vec{E} \cdot d\vec{a} = \frac{1}{\epsilon_0} \int \rho \, dv$ → Gauss's law of electrostatics (IV)
D. $\oint \vec{B} \cdot d\vec{l} = \mu_0 I$ → Ampere's circuital law (I)

subject: Physics
solution:
The speed of electromagnetic wave in free space is given by

$\mathrm{C}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mu_{0} \epsilon_{0}}}$

In medium, $\mathbf{v}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{k} \in_{0} \mu_{0}}} \ldots$ (ii)

Dividing equation (i) by(ii), we get $\therefore \frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{V}}=\sqrt{\mathrm{k}}=\mathrm{n}$

$\frac{1}{2} \epsilon_{0} \mathrm{E}_{0}^{2} \mathrm{C}=$ intensity $=\frac{1}{2} \epsilon_{0} \mathrm{kE}^{2} \mathrm{v}$

$\therefore \mathrm{E}_{0}^{2} \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{kE}^{2} \mathrm{v}$

$\Rightarrow \frac{E_{0}^{2}}{E^{2}}=\frac{k V}{C}=\frac{n^{2}}{n} \Rightarrow \frac{E_{0}}{E}=\sqrt{n}$

similarly

$\frac{\mathrm{B}_{0}^{2} \mathrm{C}}{2 \mu_{0}}=\frac{\mathrm{B}^{2} \mathrm{v}}{2 \mu_{0}} \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{B}_{0}}{\mathrm{~B}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{n}}}$

subject: Physics
solution:
Power of the source, $P = 15 \text{ kW} = 15 \times 10^3 \text{ J/s}$

Number of photons emitted per second, $n = 2.5 \times 10^{22} \text{ s}^{-1}$

Energy of one photon, $E = \dfrac{P}{n} = \dfrac{15 \times 10^3}{2.5 \times 10^{22}} = 6 \times 10^{-19} \text{ J}$

Using $E = \dfrac{hc}{\lambda}$, we get $\lambda = \dfrac{hc}{E}$

$\lambda = \dfrac{6.6 \times 10^{-34} \times 3 \times 10^8}{6 \times 10^{-19}} = 3.3 \times 10^{-7} \text{ m} = 330 \text{ nm}$

The wavelength $330 \text{ nm}$ lies in the ultraviolet region of the electromagnetic spectrum.

Answer: Ultraviolet

subject: Physics
solution:
EM wave intensity

$\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\text { Power }}{\text { Area }}=\frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_{0} \mathrm{E}_{0}^{2} \mathrm{c}$

[where $\mathrm{E}_{0}=$ maximum electric field] $]$

$\Rightarrow \frac{27 \times 10^{-3}}{10 \times 10^{-6}}=\frac{1}{2} \times 9 \times 10^{-12} \times \mathrm{E}_{0}^{2} \times 3 \times 10^{8}$

$\Rightarrow \mathrm{E}_{0}=\sqrt{2} \times 10^{3} \mathrm{kV} / \mathrm{m}=1.4 \mathrm{kV} / \mathrm{m}$

subject: Physics
solution:
At every instant, the ratio of the magnitude of the electric field to the magnitude of the magnetic field in an electromagnetic wave equals the speed of light.

Therefore, EB=c

⇒EB=3×108

⇒B=E3×108=9.63×108

⇒B=3.2×10-8 T

Now, direction of magnetic field can be calculated as

B^=v^×E^

=i^×j^=k^

So, B→=3.2×10-8k^T

c=E0B0⇒B0=E0c

And magnetic intensity is given by,

H0=B0μ0⇒H0=E0cμ0

Also, E→ and B→ are perpendicular to each other.

Therefore,

Hy0=301.63×108×4π×10-7=0.8 A m-1Hx0=452.43×108×4π×10-7=1.2 A m-1

Hence, C is the answer.

An optical telecommunication is communication at a distance using light to carry information. It can be performed visually or by using electronic devices.

The wavelength of the microwaves are greater than that of the common optical light waves.

Hence, the Assertion is not correct. But, because of the small wavelength, the electromagnetic waves are more energetic than the microwaves. Hence, the reason is true.
